evals.prompt:
Break this PRD into Jira tickets for the happy project:

# Dark Mode Toggle

## Overview
Add a dark mode toggle to the user settings page. Users should be able to switch between light and dark themes, and their preference should persist across sessions.

## Requirements
- Add a toggle switch in the Settings page under "Appearance"
- When toggled, the entire app should switch themes immediately (no page reload)
- Persist the user's preference in their profile (backend)
- Default to the system preference on first visit
- Support the existing color tokens in our design system (we already have dark variants defined in Tailwind)
- Update the marketing site's settings screenshot to show the new toggle

## Design
Figma link: [mock showing toggle placement in settings]

## Technical Notes
- We use Tailwind's `dark:` variant classes
- User preferences are stored in the `user_settings` table
- The marketing site is a separate Next.js app in the `marketing/` repo
evals.expected_output: Should create an epic with 3-4 right-sized tickets: frontend toggle+theming, backend preference API, marketing screenshot update. Should NOT create tiny tickets like 'add CSS class' or 'create DB column'. Should detect that frontend and marketing are independent but both backend work blocks frontend persistence.

evals.prompt:
I need to break this into tickets, use the happy project:

# Referral Program Launch

## Overview
Launch a referral program where existing users can invite friends. When a referred friend completes their first purchase, both the referrer and referee get a $25 credit.

## Requirements

### User-Facing
- Referral dashboard in user account showing: unique referral link, number of referrals, credits earned
- Referred users see "Referred by [Name]" on the checkout page with a $25 discount applied automatically
- Email notifications: to referrer when friend signs up, when friend purchases, when credit is applied
- Referral link format: happysleep.com/r/{code}

### Backend
- Generate unique referral codes per user
- Track referral chain: who referred whom, signup date, purchase date
- Credit system: $25 credit to both parties after referee's first purchase
- Fraud prevention: limit 10 referrals per user per month, no self-referrals, same household detection via address matching
- Referral analytics: conversion rates, revenue impact, top referrers

### Operations
- Set up referral tracking in our analytics pipeline (Segment events)
- Configure monitoring/alerting for referral credit payouts exceeding $10k/month
- Update customer support runbook with referral FAQ

### Marketing
- Launch email campaign to existing users announcing the program
- Add referral CTA to post-purchase confirmation page
- Social sharing meta tags for referral links (OG image, title, description)

## Open Questions
- Should credits expire? If so, after how long?
- Do we need approval workflow for payouts over a certain threshold?
- How do we handle referrals for the insurance flow vs self-pay?
evals.expected_output: Should create an epic with 6-10 tickets spanning Frontend, Backend, Ops, Marketing areas. Should properly detect dependencies (backend referral system blocks frontend dashboard; backend blocks marketing referral links). Should include the open questions from the PRD on relevant tickets only (not all tickets). Should NOT create micro-tickets like 'create referral code table' separately from the referral backend work.

evals.prompt:
Can you turn this into Jira tickets? Use happy project.

# SMS Notifications

We want to add SMS notifications to our platform. Users should get text messages for important events. We're thinking Twilio but open to alternatives.

## What we want
- Send SMS for key events (appointment reminders, order confirmations, shipping updates)
- Users can opt in/out of SMS
- Need to be TCPA compliant

That's pretty much it for now. Let me know if you have questions.
evals.expected_output: Should identify this PRD as vague and generate meaningful open questions: what events exactly trigger SMS? What's the opt-in flow (checkout, settings, both)? Do we need two-way SMS? What's the budget/volume expectation? Should still produce a reasonable ticket breakdown (backend Twilio integration, frontend opt-in UI, compliance/ops work) but with prominent questions sections. Should NOT just guess at details the PRD doesn't specify.
